_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**No new EPR/producer registration schemes arise directly from this May 2026 Queensland legislative update package.** The legislative instruments involved do not impose a registration obligation on freight forwarders as a distinct regulated class.

However, the following registration and reporting obligations are relevant to the workspace's Queensland operations:

**Biosecurity Regulation 2016 (pending replacement):** The Regulation sets out reporting obligations for the presence of prohibited matter (e.g., fire ants, varroa mite, polyphagous shot-hole borer, cattle ticks) that apply to all persons — including logistics operators — who encounter such matter during freight operations. There is no formal registration requirement for carriers under the current Regulation, but a duty to notify biosecurity authorities upon discovery. The replacement Regulation (post-sunset review) may alter these obligations; the scope and format of any new reporting requirements is not yet published, as the consultation closed 29 May 2026 and the replacement instrument is yet to be made. Data fields the workspace should currently maintain include: cargo type, origin, route, biosecurity zone crossings, and any incidents or observations of regulated matter. *Source: Biosecurity Act 2014 s 195; Biosecurity Regulation 2016; DPI Queensland. https://www.dpi.qld.gov.au/business-priorities/biosecurity/policy-legislation-regulation/biosecurity-act-2014*

**Trusts Act 2025:** Trustees of Queensland trusts (including any operational trust structures the workspace holds or administers in Queensland) are now subject to explicit statutory record-keeping and reporting obligations under the Act. The Act imposes a codified duty to maintain proper records and, in appropriate cases, provide information to beneficiaries upon request. No external registration with a regulator is required, but internal governance documentation must be updated to reflect the new statutory baseline. The supporting Trusts Regulation 2026 provides transitional and operational detail. *Source: Trusts Act 2025 (Qld), Queensland Parliamentary Counsel, commenced 28 April 2026; Trusts Regulation 2026. https://www.legislation.qld.gov.au/view/whole/html/inforce/2026-05-02/act-2025-008*
